story.text: As a session working deep in a module, I need the harness config (.claude/) and the family-neutral fence (fence/) to travel with me in always-on context — because governance that binds wherever I work belongs on the composition surface, not in folders I only meet if I happen to open them; a session can forget the paved path while standing on it.

briefing.headline: Compose the harness + fence into the always-on surface
briefing.value: An audit of the repo's eleven CLAUDE.md files against bdo's @/@@ directive (done-line 0010) found the composition surface had fallen behind: the root @-imported only five, leaving the two contracts that bind every session — .claude/ (pens, guards, freeze, merge-node) and fence/ (the registry every command-denial derives from) — to nested loading. The change wires @.claude/CLAUDE.md and @fence/CLAUDE.md into the root, and names the inclusion test in prose so the surface stops drifting: a module joins the @-surface when its environment binds a session wherever it works.
briefing.why_now: The repo grew eleven CLAUDE.md files while the root surface stayed at five; a deep-loop session never carried the binding rules in always-on context. The audit also found Codex's parallel AGENTS.md list had drifted (named .claude/ but not fence/).
briefing.if_accepted: Governance that binds everywhere rides every session for both families: Claude composes @.claude + @fence; Codex's manual-read list is brought to the same always-on set (its .codex/ fence is rendered from fence/). tests/ and pivot/ stay nested deliberately — their knowledge only matters inside them.
briefing.if_rejected: The composition surface keeps drifting from the rules it should carry, and a session can stand on the paved path while forgetting it exists.
briefing.cost_of_wrong_call: Low: docs-only; no code path changes. Reverting is editing two @-import lines back out.
briefing.mechanism: CLAUDE.md @.claude/CLAUDE.md + @fence/CLAUDE.md into the root surface; AGENTS.md brought to the same always-on set with the nested-only modules separated, so both families read the same map. Honors the existing directive rather than redirecting it.

_note: Birth record for composing the harness + fence into the always-on surface, authored to adopt PR #107 into the loop (bdo, 2026-06-13). Never mutated; verdicts are the fold over .ai-native/log/ (D-5).
